misc.title: This book is filled with a number of incredible illustrations and images I’ve hardly ever seen before. Picture 1 features illustrations of a Poké Doll, the Silph Scope, and the coin case from the Game
misc.text: This book is filled with a number of incredible illustrations and images I’ve hardly ever seen before. Picture 1 features illustrations of a Poké Doll, the Silph Scope, and the coin case from the Game Corner! Picture 2 shows off an illustrated version of Fuchsia City. We also can see exactly what the Warden’s Gold Teeth look like! Picture 3 shows off the Gengar line, illustrated by Ken Sugimori. Additionally, in the top right hand of the page, is another illustration of Gengar that is much darker. This illustration was done by Benimaru Itoh. Itoh’s illustrations are used for some other Pokémon throughout the book as well, like Pikachu (picture 4). Fun fact, these illustrations are also featured on the backside of the 1996 Pokémon Bandai Carddass Vending cards! PCStop (@PokeCardStop) The very first Pokémon Encyclopedia and Illustration Book, released only in Japan back in 1996. This was the very first physical Pokémon book with some incredible art, Pokémon Red/Green references, and interviews from Game Freak’s staff. This feels like a true piece of Pokémon history that I’m extremely grateful to now have in my collection ❤️ — https://nitter.net/PokeCardStop/status/2038634016174170374#m

misc.title: La bajada de audiencia en Francia debe ser porque el coche de Prost este año no tira, nada que ver tampoco con el reglamento. Holiness (@F1BigData) In France, the audience also fell by 43% compared wi
misc.text: La bajada de audiencia en Francia debe ser porque el coche de Prost este año no tira, nada que ver tampoco con el reglamento. Holiness (@F1BigData) In France, the audience also fell by 43% compared with 2025 2026: 404,000 2025: 705,000 📊@Monascope7 — https://nitter.net/F1BigData/status/2038639161863737419#m
